@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@
+package config
+
+import (
+	"testing"
+
+	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/cache"
+	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/maps"
+	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/runtime/mock"
+	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/segments"
+	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/template"
+	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
+)
+
+func TestSegmentCache(t *testing.T) {
+	template.Cache = &cache.Template{
+		Segments: maps.NewConcurrent[any](),
+	}
+	defer cache.DeleteAll(cache.Device)
+
+	env := new(mock.Environment)
+	env.On("Pwd").Return("/tmp")
+
+	segment := &Segment{
+		Type: TEXT,
+		Cache: &Cache{
+			Strategy: Folder,
+			Duration: cache.Duration("10m"),
+		},
+		Alias: "my_text_segment",
+		env:   env,
+	}
+
+	textWriter := &segments.Text{}
+	textWriter.Init(nil, nil)
+	textWriter.SetText("Hello, Cache!")
+	segment.writer = textWriter
+	segment.name = "my_text_segment"
+
+	segment.setCache()
+
+	newSegment := &Segment{
+		Type: TEXT,
+		Cache: &Cache{
+			Strategy: Folder,
+			Duration: cache.Duration("10m"),
+		},
+		Alias: "my_text_segment",
+		env:   env,
+	}
+	newSegment.name = "my_text_segment"
+
+	newTextWriter := &segments.Text{}
+	newTextWriter.Init(nil, nil)
+	newSegment.writer = newTextWriter
+
+	restored := newSegment.restoreCache()
+
+	assert.True(t, restored, "Cache should be restored")
+	assert.NotNil(t, newSegment.writer, "Writer should be restored")
+	assert.IsType(t, &segments.Text{}, newSegment.writer, "Writer should be of type *segments.Text")
+	if newSegment.writer != nil {
+		assert.Equal(t, "Hello, Cache!", newSegment.writer.Text(), "Restored text should match")
+	}
+
+	// Test legacy cache (string value)
+	legacySegment := &Segment{
+		Type: TEXT,
+		Cache: &Cache{
+			Strategy: Device,
+			Duration: cache.Duration("10m"),
+		},
+		Alias: "legacy_segment",
+		env:   env,
+	}
+	legacySegment.name = "legacy_segment"
+
+	// Initialize writer so cacheKeyAndStore can compute the key
+	legacyWriter := &segments.Text{}
+	legacyWriter.Init(nil, nil)
+	legacySegment.writer = legacyWriter
+
+	// Derive the key using the same logic as production code
+	legacyKey, legacyStore := legacySegment.cacheKeyAndStore()
+	cache.Set(legacyStore, legacyKey, "legacy_json_string", cache.Duration("10m"))
+
+	// Should return false and delete the key
+	restoredLegacy := legacySegment.restoreCache()
+	assert.False(t, restoredLegacy, "Legacy cache should not be restored")
+
+	// Verify key is gone
+	_, found := cache.Get[string](legacyStore, legacyKey)
+	assert.False(t, found, "Legacy key should be removed")
+}

93	// segmentAlias is used to avoid recursion during unmarshaling
94	type segmentAlias Segment
95	
96	// segmentAux is a helper struct that captures the legacy 'properties' field
97	type segmentAux struct {
98		Properties options.Map `json:"properties,omitempty" yaml:"properties,omitempty" toml:"properties,omitempty"`
99		*segmentAlias
100	}
101	
102	func (segment *Segment) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
103		aux := &segmentAux{
104			segmentAlias: (*segmentAlias)(segment),
105		}
106	
107		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, aux); err != nil {
108			return err
109		}
110	
111		// Migrate 'properties' to 'options' if present
112		if len(aux.Properties) > 0 && len(segment.Options) == 0 {
113			segment.Options = aux.Properties
114		}
115	
116		return nil
117	}
118	
119	func (segment *Segment) UnmarshalYAML(node *yaml.Node) error {
120		// Decode into a map to handle field renaming
121		var raw map[string]any
122		if err := node.Decode(&raw); err != nil {
123			return err
124		}
125	
126		// If 'properties' exists and 'options' doesn't, rename it
127		if props, hasProps := raw["properties"]; hasProps {
128			if _, hasOptions := raw["options"]; !hasOptions {
129				raw["options"] = props
130				delete(raw, "properties")
131			}
132		}
133	
134		// Re-encode and decode into the struct
135		modifiedNode := &yaml.Node{}
136		if err := modifiedNode.Encode(raw); err != nil {
137			return err
138		}
139	
140		return modifiedNode.Decode((*segmentAlias)(segment))
141	}
142	
143	// MigratePropertiesToOptions migrates the deprecated Properties field to Options.
144	// This is needed for TOML configs since go-toml/v2 doesn't support custom unmarshalers.
145	func (segment *Segment) MigratePropertiesToOptions() {
146		if len(segment.Properties) > 0 && len(segment.Options) == 0 {
147			segment.Options = segment.Properties
148			segment.Properties = nil
149		}
150	}
151	
152	func (segment *Segment) Name() string {
153		if len(segment.name) != 0 {
154			return segment.name
155		}
156	
157		name := segment.Alias
158		if name == "" {
159			name = c.Title(language.English).String(string(segment.Type))
160		}
161	
162		segment.name = name
163		return name
164	}
165	
166	func (segment *Segment) Execute(env runtime.Environment) {
167		// segment timings for debug purposes
168		var start time.Time
169		if env.Flags().Debug {
170			start = time.Now()
171			segment.NameLength = len(segment.Name())
172			defer func() {
173				segment.Duration = time.Since(start)
174			}()
175		}
176	
177		defer segment.evaluateNeeds()
178	
179		err := segment.MapSegmentWithWriter(env)
180		if err != nil || !segment.shouldIncludeFolder() {
181			return
182		}
183	
184		log.Debugf("segment: %s", segment.Name())
185	
186		if segment.isToggled() {
187			return
188		}
189	
190		if segment.restoreData() {
191			return
192		}
193	
194		cacheRestored := segment.restoreCache()
195		if cacheRestored && !env.Flags().Streaming {
196			return
197		}
198	
199		if shouldHideForWidth(segment.env, segment.MinWidth, segment.MaxWidth) {
200			return
201		}
202	
203		defer func() {
204			if segment.Enabled {
205				template.Cache.AddSegmentData(segment.Name(), segment.writer)
206			}
207		}()
208	
209		// Only segments with a timeout can ever be killed via
210		// KillGoroutineChildren (see prompt/segments.go executeSegmentWithTimeout),
211		// so only those need a Job object to track/terminate their child
212		// processes. Skipping this for the common case (no timeout configured)
213		// avoids two syscalls + a map insert per segment per prompt.
214		if segment.Timeout > 0 {
215			if err := runjobs.CreateJobForGoroutine(segment.Name()); err != nil {
216				log.Errorf("failed to create job for goroutine (segment: %s): %v", segment.Name(), err)
217			}
218	
219			// Release the Job object (Windows) once this goroutine is done
220			// spawning/waiting on children. cmd.Run blocks until its child exits,
221			// so by the time Execute returns - on any path below, including a
222			// panic unwind - no process we intended to keep alive is still
223			// assigned to the job, making it safe to close despite
224			// JOB_OBJECT_LIMIT_KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE. If the timeout path already
225			// terminated/closed the job concurrently, this is a no-op; see the
226			// ownership invariant documented on jobs.CloseGoroutineJob.
227			defer runjobs.CloseGoroutineJob()
228		}
229	
230		segment.Enabled = segment.writer.Enabled()
231		segment.evaluated = true
232	}
233	
234	func (segment *Segment) Render(index int, force bool) bool {
235		// Allow pending segments to render (they'll show "..." text)
236		if !segment.Pending && !segment.Enabled && !force {
237			return segment.renderFallback(index)
238		}
239	
240		if force {
241			segment.Force = true
242		}
243	
244		// Foreground/background may be overridden directly (e.g. color cycling) between
245		// render passes, so the memoized values must not survive across calls to Render.
246		segment.foregroundResolved = false
247		segment.backgroundResolved = false
248	
249		segment.writer.SetIndex(index)
250	
251		text := segment.string()
252	
253		// Only update Enabled if segment is NOT pending (avoid race with Execute goroutine)
254		if !segment.Pending {
255			segment.Enabled = segment.Force || strings.ContainsFunc(text, func(r rune) bool { return r != ' ' })
256	
257			if !segment.Enabled {
258				template.Cache.RemoveSegmentData(segment.Name())
259				return false
260			}
261		}
262	
263		segment.SetText(text)
264		segment.setCache()
265	
266		// We do this to make `.Text` available for a cross-segment reference in an extra prompt.
267		template.Cache.AddSegmentData(segment.Name(), segment.writer)
268	
269		return true
270	}
271	
272	// renderFallback attempts to render FallbackTemplate when a segment would
273	// otherwise be hidden because its writer's Enabled() returned false. It
274	// requires the writer to have completed its evaluation; segments hidden for other
275	// reasons (toggled off, folder include/exclude, width constraints, or a
276	// writer mapping error) never set evaluated, so they keep the silent-omission
277	// behavior. Segments killed by their timeout are excluded via Killed rather
278	// than evaluated, because their Execute goroutine keeps running after the
279	// kill and may still complete the evaluation before rendering starts.
280	func (segment *Segment) renderFallback(index int) bool {
281		if segment.FallbackTemplate == "" || !segment.evaluated || segment.Killed {
282			return false
283		}
284	
285		text, err := template.Render(segment.FallbackTemplate, segment.writer)
286		if err != nil {
287			text = err.Error()
288		}
289	
290		if !strings.ContainsFunc(text, func(r rune) bool { return r != ' ' }) {
291			return false
292		}
293	
294		// Foreground/background may be overridden directly (e.g. color cycling) between
295		// render passes, so the memoized values must not survive across calls to Render.
296		segment.foregroundResolved = false
297		segment.backgroundResolved = false
298	
299		segment.writer.SetIndex(index)
300		segment.Enabled = true
301		segment.SetText(text)
302	
303		// Intentionally skip setCache(): the writer is zero/partially hydrated
304		// here, and caching it would make restoreCache() later resurrect a
305		// disabled writer as enabled while rendering the main template against
306		// empty data.
307		template.Cache.AddSegmentData(segment.Name(), segment.writer)
308	
309		return true
310	}
311	
312	func (segment *Segment) Text() string {
313		return segment.writer.Text()
314	}
315	
316	func (segment *Segment) SetText(text string) {
317		segment.writer.SetText(text)
318	}
319	
320	func (segment *Segment) ResolveForeground() color.Ansi {
321		if segment.foregroundResolved {
322			return segment.foregroundCache
323		}
324	
325		if len(segment.ForegroundTemplates) != 0 {
326			match := segment.ForegroundTemplates.FirstMatch(segment.writer, segment.Foreground.String())
327			segment.Foreground = color.Ansi(match)
328		}
329	
330		segment.foregroundCache = segment.Foreground
331		segment.foregroundResolved = true
332	
333		return segment.foregroundCache
334	}
335	
336	func (segment *Segment) ResolveBackground() color.Ansi {
337		if segment.backgroundResolved {
338			return segment.backgroundCache
339		}
340	
341		if len(segment.BackgroundTemplates) != 0 {
342			match := segment.BackgroundTemplates.FirstMatch(segment.writer, segment.Background.String())
343			segment.Background = color.Ansi(match)
344		}
345	
346		segment.backgroundCache = segment.Background
347		segment.backgroundResolved = true
348	
349		return segment.backgroundCache
350	}
351	
352	func (segment *Segment) ResolveStyle() SegmentStyle {
353		if len(segment.styleCache) != 0 {
354			return segment.styleCache
355		}
356	
357		segment.styleCache = segment.Style.resolve(segment.writer)
358	
359		return segment.styleCache
360	}
361	
362	func (segment *Segment) IsPowerline() bool {
363		style := segment.ResolveStyle()
364		return style == Powerline || style == Accordion
365	}
366	
367	func (segment *Segment) HasEmptyDiamondAtEnd() bool {
368		if segment.ResolveStyle() != Diamond {
369			return false
370		}
371	
372		return segment.TrailingDiamond == ""
373	}
374	
375	func (segment *Segment) hasCache() bool {
376		return segment.Cache != nil && !segment.Cache.Duration.IsEmpty()
377	}
378	
379	// DataKey returns the identity used to look up segment data: the segment's
380	// alias if set, falling back to its type.
381	func (segment *Segment) DataKey() string {
382		if segment.Alias != "" {
383			return segment.Alias
384		}
385	
386		return string(segment.Type)
387	}
388	
389	// Writer returns the segment's underlying SegmentWriter.
390	func (segment *Segment) Writer() SegmentWriter {
391		return segment.writer
392	}
393	
394	func (segment *Segment) isToggled() bool {
395		togglesMap, OK := cache.Get[map[string]bool](cache.Session, cache.TOGGLECACHE)
396		if !OK || len(togglesMap) == 0 {
397			log.Debug("no toggles found")
398			return false
399		}
400	
401		if togglesMap[segment.DataKey()] {
402			log.Debugf("segment toggled off: %s", segment.Name())
403			return true
404		}
405	
406		return false
407	}
408	
409	func (segment *Segment) restoreCache() bool {
410		if !segment.hasCache() {
411			return false
412		}
413	
414		key, store := segment.cacheKeyAndStore()
415	
416		data, OK := cache.Get[any](store, key)
417		if !OK {
418			log.Debugf("no cache found for segment: %s, key: %s", segment.Name(), key)
419			return false
420		}
421	
422		switch v := data.(type) {
423		case SegmentWriter:
424			segment.writer = v
425			segment.Enabled = true
426			template.Cache.AddSegmentData(segment.Name(), segment.writer)
427			log.Debug("restored segment from cache: ", segment.Name())
428			segment.restored = true
429			return true
430		case string:
431			// legacy JSON cache entry, remove it so it gets re-cached with the new format
432			log.Debugf("removing legacy cache key: %s", key)
433			cache.Delete(store, key)
434			return false
435		default:
436			log.Debugf("unexpected cache type for segment: %s, key: %s", segment.Name(), key)
437			cache.Delete(store, key)
438			return false
439		}
440	}
441	
442	// restoreData replays a segment's writer state from the data file supplied via
443	// runtime.Flags.SegmentData, bypassing the real runtime entirely. This lets
444	// segments render from a recorded fixture instead of probing the environment.
445	func (segment *Segment) restoreData() bool {
446		data, OK := segment.env.Flags().SegmentData[segment.DataKey()]
447		if !OK {
448			return false
449		}
450	
451		err := json.Unmarshal(data, &segment.writer)
452		if err != nil {
453			log.Error(err)
454			return false
455		}
456	
457		segment.Enabled = true
458		segment.restored = true
459	
460		template.Cache.AddSegmentData(segment.Name(), segment.writer)
461	
462		log.Debug("restored segment from data: ", segment.Name())
463	
464		return true
465	}
466	
467	func (segment *Segment) setCache() {
468		if segment.restored || !segment.hasCache() {
469			return
470		}
471	
472		// Never cache pending state to avoid polluting cache with incomplete data
473		if segment.Pending {
474			return
475		}
476	
477		// TODO: check if we can make segmentwriter a generic Type indicator
478		// that way we can actually get the value straight from cache.Get
479		// and marchalling is obsolete
480		key, store := segment.cacheKeyAndStore()
481		cache.Set(store, key, segment.writer, segment.Cache.Duration)
482	}
483	
484	func (segment *Segment) cacheKeyAndStore() (string, cache.Store) {
485		format := "segment_cache_%s"
486		switch segment.Cache.Strategy {
487		case Session:
488			return fmt.Sprintf(format, segment.Name()), cache.Session
489		case Device:
490			return fmt.Sprintf(format, segment.Name()), cache.Device
491		case Folder:
492			fallthrough
493		default:
494			return fmt.Sprintf(format, strings.Join([]string{segment.Name(), segment.folderKey()}, "_")), cache.Device
495		}
496	}
497	
498	func (segment *Segment) folderKey() string {
499		key, ok := segment.writer.CacheKey()
500		if !ok {
501			return segment.env.Pwd()
502		}
503	
504		return key
505	}
506	
507	func (segment *Segment) string() string {
508		// Use simple pending text if segment is still pending
509		if segment.Pending {
510			if segment.Placeholder != "" {
511				return segment.Placeholder
512			}
513	
514			return "..."
515		}
516	
517		result := segment.Templates.Resolve(segment.writer, "", segment.TemplatesLogic)
518		if len(result) != 0 {
519			return result
520		}
521	
522		if segment.Template == "" {
523			segment.Template = segment.writer.Template()
524		}
525	
526		text, err := template.Render(segment.Template, segment.writer)
527		if err != nil {
528			return err.Error()
529		}
530	
531		return text
532	}
533	
534	func (segment *Segment) shouldIncludeFolder() bool {
535		if segment.env == nil {
536			return true
537		}
538	
539		cwdIncluded := segment.cwdIncluded()
540		cwdExcluded := segment.cwdExcluded()
541	
542		return cwdIncluded && !cwdExcluded
543	}
544	
545	func (segment *Segment) cwdIncluded() bool {
546		if len(segment.IncludeFolders) == 0 {
547			return true
548		}
549	
550		return segment.env.DirMatchesOneOf(segment.env.Pwd(), segment.IncludeFolders)
551	}
552	
553	func (segment *Segment) cwdExcluded() bool {
554		return segment.env.DirMatchesOneOf(segment.env.Pwd(), segment.ExcludeFolders)
555	}
556	
557	func (segment *Segment) evaluateNeeds() {
558		if segment.needsEvaluated {
559			return
560		}
561	
562		segment.needsEvaluated = true
563	
564		value := segment.Template
565		if value == "" && segment.writer != nil {
566			value = segment.writer.Template()
567		}
568	
569		if len(segment.ForegroundTemplates) != 0 {
570			value += strings.Join(segment.ForegroundTemplates, "")
571		}
572	
573		if len(segment.BackgroundTemplates) != 0 {
574			value += strings.Join(segment.BackgroundTemplates, "")
575		}
576	
577		if len(segment.Templates) != 0 {
578			value += strings.Join(segment.Templates, "")
579		}
580	
581		if segment.FallbackTemplate != "" {
582			value += segment.FallbackTemplate
583		}
584	
585		if !strings.Contains(value, ".Segments.") {
586			return
587		}
588	
589		matches := regex.FindAllNamedRegexMatch(`\.Segments\.(?P<NAME>[a-zA-Z0-9]+)`, value)
590		for _, name := range matches {
591			segmentName := name["NAME"]
592	
593			if len(name) == 0 || slices.Contains(segment.Needs, segmentName) {
594				continue
595			}
596	
597			segment.Needs = append(segment.Needs, segmentName)
598		}
599	}
600	
601	func (segment *Segment) key() any {
602		if segment.Index > 0 {
603			return segment.Index - 1
604		}
605	
606		return segment.Name()
607	}

1	package cache
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/gob"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// ErrLocked is returned by openFile when the cache file is held exclusively
10	// by another process (e.g. a Windows sharing violation that persisted past
11	// the retry window). Callers must treat this as "leave the file alone":
12	// operate purely in-memory for this run and do not recreate/truncate the
13	// file on close.
14	var ErrLocked = errors.New("cache file is locked by another process")
15	
16	func init() {
17		gob.Register(&Entry[any]{})
18		gob.Register(Template{})
19		gob.Register(SimpleTemplate{})
20		gob.Register((*Duration)(nil))
21		gob.Register(map[string]bool{})
22		gob.Register(commandPathEntry{})
23	}
24	
25	const (
26		DeviceStore = "omp.cache"
27	)
28	
29	const (
30		TEMPLATECACHE    = "template_cache"
31		TOGGLECACHE      = "toggle_cache"
32		PROMPTCOUNTCACHE = "prompt_count_cache"
33		ENGINECACHE      = "engine_cache"
34		FONTLISTCACHE    = "font_list_cache"
35		CLAUDECACHE      = "claude_cache"
36		COPILOTCLICACHE  = "copilot_cli_cache"
37	)
38	
39	type Entry[T any] struct {
40		Value     T
41		Timestamp int64
42		TTL       int
43	}
44	
45	func (c *Entry[T]) Expired() bool {
46		if c.TTL < 0 {
47			return false
48		}
49	
50		return time.Now().Unix() >= (c.Timestamp + int64(c.TTL))
51	}
52

1	package cache
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/gob"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"os"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strings"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/log"
13		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/maps"
14	)
15	
16	type store struct {
17		cache    *maps.Concurrent[*Entry[any]]
18		filePath string
19		dirty    bool
20		persist  bool
21		// locked is set when the on-disk cache file could not be opened because
22		// another process holds it (e.g. Windows sharing violation that
23		// persisted past the retry window). When true, the store operates
24		// purely in-memory for the lifetime of this process: close() must not
25		// attempt to (re)create the file, since doing so would truncate the
26		// other process's data.
27		locked bool
28	}
29	
30	var (
31		session *store
32		device  *store
33	)
34	
35	type Store string
36	
37	const (
38		Session Store  = "session"
39		Device  Store  = "device"
40		TTL     string = "ttl"
41	)
42	
43	func (s Store) new() *store {
44		return &store{
45			cache: maps.NewConcurrent[*Entry[any]](),
46		}
47	}
48	
49	// getStore returns the appropriate store based on the Store identifier
50	func (s Store) get() *store {
51		switch s {
52		case Device:
53			if device == nil {
54				device = s.new()
55			}
56	
57			return device
58		default:
59			if session == nil {
60				session = s.new()
61			}
62	
63			return session
64		}
65	}
66	
67	// Init initializes a store with the given file path
68	func (s Store) init(filePath string, persist bool) {
69		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s), filePath)
70	
71		store := s.get()
72		store.cache = maps.NewConcurrent[*Entry[any]]()
73		store.filePath = filepath.Join(Path(), filePath)
74		store.persist = persist
75		store.dirty = false
76		store.locked = false
77	
78		reader, err := openFile(store.filePath)
79		if err != nil {
80			if errors.Is(err, ErrLocked) {
81				// Another process holds the file. Leave it alone: run this
82				// session purely in-memory, don't mark dirty, don't recreate
83				// on close.
84				log.Debugf("(%s) cache file is locked, running in-memory only", string(s))
85				store.locked = true
86				return
87			}
88	
89			// set to dirty so we create it on close
90			log.Error(err)
91			store.dirty = true
92			return
93		}
94	
95		defer reader.Close()
96	
97		var list maps.Simple[*Entry[any]]
98	
99		dec := gob.NewDecoder(reader)
100		if err := dec.Decode(&list); err != nil {
101			log.Error(err)
102			// If gob decoding fails, the cache file might be from the old format
103			// Set dirty to true so we recreate it in gob format
104			store.dirty = true
105			return
106		}
107	
108		for key, entry := range list {
109			if entry.Expired() {
110				log.Debugf("(%s) skipping expired key: %s", string(s), key)
111				continue
112			}
113	
114			log.Debugf("(%s) loading %s", string(s), key)
115			store.cache.Set(key, entry)
116		}
117	}
118	
119	// touchSessionFile updates the session file's modification time if it's older than 1 hour.
120	// This prevents stale session cache files from being cleaned up while reducing steady-state overhead.
121	func touchSessionFile(filePath string) {
122		info, err := os.Stat(filePath)
123		if err != nil {
124			return
125		}
126	
127		if time.Since(info.ModTime()) <= time.Hour {
128			return
129		}
130	
131		if err := os.Chtimes(filePath, time.Now(), time.Now()); err != nil {
132			log.Error(err)
133		}
134	}
135	
136	func (s Store) close() {
137		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s))
138	
139		store := s.get()
140		if store == nil || store.locked || !store.persist || !store.dirty {
141			if s == Session && store != nil && !store.locked && store.filePath != "" {
142				touchSessionFile(store.filePath)
143			}
144	
145			log.Debugf("(%s) not persisting", string(s))
146			return
147		}
148	
149		cache := store.cache.ToSimple()
150	
151		file, err := openFileForWrite(store.filePath)
152		if err != nil {
153			if errors.Is(err, ErrLocked) {
154				// Became locked between init and close (e.g. another process
155				// started writing meanwhile) — do not recreate/truncate.
156				log.Debugf("(%s) cache file locked on close, skipping persist", string(s))
157				return
158			}
159	
160			log.Error(err)
161			return
162		}
163	
164		defer func() {
165			if err := file.Close(); err != nil {
166				log.Error(err)
167			}
168		}()
169	
170		enc := gob.NewEncoder(file)
171		if err := enc.Encode(cache); err != nil {
172			log.Error(err)
173		}
174	}
175	
176	// Get retrieves a typed value from the specified store
177	func Get[T any](s Store, key string) (T, bool) {
178		var zero T
179		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s), key)
180	
181		store := s.get()
182		if store == nil {
183			log.Debugf("(%s) store is nil", string(s))
184			return zero, false
185		}
186	
187		entry, found := store.cache.Get(key)
188		if !found {
189			log.Debugf("(%s) key not found: %s", string(s), key)
190			return zero, false
191		}
192	
193		if entry.Expired() {
194			log.Debugf("(%s) key expired: %s", string(s), key)
195			store.cache.Delete(key)
196			store.dirty = true
197			return zero, false
198		}
199	
200		// Type assertion to get the typed value
201		if typed, ok := entry.Value.(T); ok {
202			log.Debugf("(%s) found entry: %s - %v", string(s), key, typed)
203			return typed, true
204		}
205	
206		log.Error(fmt.Errorf("(%s) type mismatch for key: %s. Got %T, expected %T", string(s), key, entry.Value, zero))
207		return zero, false
208	}
209	
210	// Set stores a typed value in the specified store
211	func Set[T any](s Store, key string, value T, duration Duration) {
212		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s), key)
213	
214		store := s.get()
215		if store == nil {
216			log.Debugf("(%s) store is nil", string(s))
217			return
218		}
219	
220		seconds := duration.Seconds()
221		if seconds == 0 {
222			return
223		}
224	
225		log.Debugf("(%s) setting entry: %s - %v with duration: %s", string(s), key, value, string(duration))
226	
227		store.cache.Set(key, &Entry[any]{
228			Value:     value,
229			Timestamp: time.Now().Unix(),
230			TTL:       seconds,
231		})
232	
233		store.dirty = true
234	}
235	
236	// Delete removes a key from the specified store
237	func Delete(s Store, key string) {
238		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s), key)
239	
240		store := s.get()
241		if store == nil {
242			log.Debugf("(%s) store is nil", string(s))
243			return
244		}
245	
246		log.Debugf("(%s) deleting key: %s", string(s), key)
247		store.cache.Delete(key)
248		store.dirty = true
249	}
250	
251	func DeleteAll(s Store) {
252		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s))
253	
254		store := s.get()
255		if store == nil {
256			log.Debugf("(%s) store is nil", string(s))
257			return
258		}
259	
260		store.cache = maps.NewConcurrent[*Entry[any]]()
261		store.dirty = true
262	}
263	
264	func Print(s Store) string {
265		defer log.Trace(time.Now(), string(s))
266	
267		store := s.get()
268		if store == nil {
269			return fmt.Sprintf("Store %s is nil", string(s))
270		}
271	
272		cache := store.cache.ToSimple()
273		if len(cache) == 0 {
274			return fmt.Sprintf("Store %s is empty", string(s))
275		}
276	
277		var builder strings.Builder
278	
279		for key, entry := range cache {
280			builder.WriteString("\n")
281	
282			if entry.Expired() {
283				fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "Key: %s [EXPIRED]\n", key)
284				builder.WriteString("\n")
285				continue
286			}
287	
288			var ttlInfo string
289			if entry.TTL < 0 {
290				ttlInfo = "never expires"
291			}
292			if entry.TTL >= 0 {
293				expiresAt := time.Unix(entry.Timestamp+int64(entry.TTL), 0)
294				ttlInfo = fmt.Sprintf("expires at %s", expiresAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05"))
295			}
296	
297			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "Key: %s\n", key)
298			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "  Value: %s\n", fmt.Sprintf("%#v", entry.Value))
299			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "  Type: %T\n", entry.Value)
300			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "  Created: %s\n", time.Unix(entry.Timestamp, 0).Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05"))
301			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "  TTL: %s\n", ttlInfo)
302		}
303	
304		return builder.String()
305	}

package cache

import (
	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/maps"
)

type Template struct {
	Segments *maps.Concurrent[any]
	SimpleTemplate
}

type SimpleTemplate struct {
	SegmentsCache maps.Simple[any]
	Var           maps.Simple[any]
	PWD           string
	Folder        string
	PSWD          string
	UserName      string
	HostName      string
	ShellVersion  string
	Shell         string
	AbsolutePWD   string
	OS            string
	Version       string
	PromptCount   int
	SHLVL         int
	Jobs          int
	Code          int
	WSL           bool
	Root          bool
	Interrupted   bool
}

func (t *Template) AddSegmentData(key string, value any) {
	t.Segments.Set(key, value)
}

func (t *Template) RemoveSegmentData(key string) {
	t.Segments.Delete(key)
}

1	package template
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"strconv"
6		"strings"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/build"
10		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/cache"
11		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/log"
12		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/maps"
13		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/runtime"
14		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/runtime/path"
15	)
16	
17	var (
18		Cache *cache.Template
19	
20		// routedEnvDataKeys are env-section keys the CLI layer already routes into
21		// runtime.Flags before Init runs (see runtime.Flags.EnvData), so by the
22		// time loadCache runs, env.Pwd()/StatusCodes()/ExecutionTime() already
23		// reflect the flag-precedence-resolved value (CLI flag > data file >
24		// live). They must never be applied a second time here, or a raw file
25		// value could clobber that resolved value. A key belongs here if and only
26		// if applyDataFile routes it - listing one it does not route would instead
27		// make that key unsettable from a data file.
28		routedEnvDataKeys = []string{"PWD", "Code", "ExecutionTime", "PipeStatus", "Interrupted"}
29	)
30	
31	func loadCache(vars maps.Simple[any], aliases *maps.Config) {
32		if !env.Flags().IsPrimary {
33			// Load the template cache for a non-primary prompt before rendering any templates.
34			if OK := restoreCache(); OK {
35				// Interrupted is a per-invocation flag, never a cached value: the
36				// restored blob was saved by the primary prompt (never interrupted),
37				// so it must be refreshed from the current flags.
38				Cache.Interrupted = env.Flags().Interrupted
39				return
40			}
41		}
42	
43		// Build fully before assigning: a long-lived process (serve) rebuilds
44		// this per prompt while goroutines from an abandoned render cycle may
45		// still read the global - they must only ever observe a complete object.
46		tmpl := new(cache.Template)
47	
48		tmpl.Root = env.Root()
49		tmpl.Shell = env.Shell()
50		tmpl.ShellVersion = env.Flags().ShellVersion
51		tmpl.Code, _ = env.StatusCodes()
52		tmpl.Interrupted = env.Flags().Interrupted
53		tmpl.WSL = env.IsWsl()
54		tmpl.Segments = maps.NewConcurrent[any]()
55		tmpl.PromptCount = env.Flags().PromptCount
56		tmpl.Var = make(map[string]any)
57		tmpl.Jobs = env.Flags().JobCount
58		tmpl.Version = build.Version
59	
60		if vars != nil {
61			tmpl.Var = vars
62		}
63	
64		pwd := env.Pwd()
65		tmpl.PWD = path.ReplaceHomeDirPrefixWithTilde(pwd)
66	
67		tmpl.AbsolutePWD = pwd
68		if env.IsWsl() {
69			tmpl.AbsolutePWD, _ = env.RunCommand("wslpath", "-m", pwd)
70		}
71	
72		env.Flags().AbsolutePWD = tmpl.AbsolutePWD
73		tmpl.PSWD = env.Flags().PSWD
74	
75		tmpl.Folder = path.Base(pwd)
76		if env.GOOS() == runtime.WINDOWS && strings.HasSuffix(tmpl.Folder, ":") {
77			tmpl.Folder += `\`
78		}
79	
80		tmpl.UserName = env.User()
81		if host, err := env.Host(); err == nil {
82			tmpl.HostName = host
83		}
84	
85		goos := env.GOOS()
86		tmpl.OS = goos
87		if goos == runtime.LINUX {
88			tmpl.OS = env.Platform()
89		}
90	
91		val := env.Getenv("SHLVL")
92		if shlvl, err := strconv.Atoi(val); err == nil {
93			tmpl.SHLVL = shlvl
94		}
95	
96		overlayEnvData(tmpl)
97	
98		// Alias mapping must apply to a data-provided value exactly as it does to
99		// a live one, so it runs after the overlay, on whichever value won.
100		tmpl.Shell = aliases.GetShellName(tmpl.Shell)
101		tmpl.UserName = aliases.GetUserName(tmpl.UserName)
102		tmpl.HostName = aliases.GetHostName(tmpl.HostName)
103	
104		Cache = tmpl
105	}
106	
107	// overlayEnvData merges the data file's env section onto an already-built
108	// template cache. It only touches keys present in the data, so anything not
109	// specified keeps its live value. Keys the CLI layer already routed into
110	// runtime.Flags (see routedEnvDataKeys) are stripped first so a raw file
111	// value can never clobber the flag-precedence-resolved value. Failures are
112	// logged, never fatal - the prompt renders with live values regardless.
113	func overlayEnvData(tmpl *cache.Template) {
114		data := env.Flags().EnvData
115		if len(data) == 0 {
116			return
117		}
118	
119		var fields map[string]json.RawMessage
120		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &fields); err != nil {
121			log.Error(err)
122			return
123		}
124	
125		for _, key := range routedEnvDataKeys {
126			delete(fields, key)
127		}
128	
129		// SegmentsCache/Segments are internal cache plumbing, not user-facing
130		// template data; a data file must never be able to overwrite them. Var
131		// (template vars) is left alone - overriding it from the data file is
132		// intentional and useful.
133		delete(fields, "SegmentsCache")
134		delete(fields, "Segments")
135	
136		overlay, err := json.Marshal(fields)
137		if err != nil {
138			log.Error(err)
139			return
140		}
141	
142		if err := json.Unmarshal(overlay, &tmpl.SimpleTemplate); err != nil {
143			log.Error(err)
144		}
145	}
146	
147	func restoreCache() bool {
148		defer log.Trace(time.Now())
149	
150		val, OK := cache.Get[cache.SimpleTemplate](cache.Session, cache.TEMPLATECACHE)
151		if !OK {
152			return false
153		}
154	
155		Cache = new(cache.Template)
156		Cache.SimpleTemplate = val
157		Cache.Segments = Cache.SegmentsCache.ToConcurrent()
158	
159		return true
160	}
161	
162	func SaveCache() {
163		// only store this when in a primary prompt
164		// and when we have any extra prompt in the config
165		canSave := env.Flags().IsPrimary && env.Flags().HasExtra
166		if !canSave {
167			return
168		}
169	
170		Cache.SegmentsCache = Cache.Segments.ToSimple()
171	
172		cache.Set(cache.Session, cache.TEMPLATECACHE, &Cache.SimpleTemplate, cache.ONEDAY)
173	}
174

1	package template
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"errors"
6		"reflect"
7		"sort"
8		"strings"
9		"text/template"
10		"unicode"
11		"unicode/utf8"
12	
13		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/cache"
14		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/generics"
15		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/log"
16	)
17	
18	type Data any
19	
20	type context struct {
21		Data
22		Getenv func(string) string
23		cache.Template
24	}
25	
26	func (c *context) init(t *Text) {
27		c.Data = t.context
28		c.Getenv = env.Getenv
29		c.Template = *Cache
30	}
31	
32	var (
33		renderPool *generics.Pool[*renderer]
34	)
35	
36	// renderer holds only per-render mutable state: an output buffer and a context.
37	// The parsed template is now looked up from the shared parsedTemplates cache.
38	type renderer struct {
39		context *context
40		buffer  bytes.Buffer
41	}
42	
43	func (t *renderer) release() {
44		t.buffer.Reset()
45		t.context.Data = nil
46		renderPool.Put(t)
47	}
48	
49	// templateCacheKey returns the key used to look up a cached *template.Template.
50	// It encodes the raw (unpatched) template text and, when context is non-nil,
51	// the reflect.Type of the context so that two different struct types whose
52	// patchTemplate output would differ (via hasField) get separate cache entries.
53	// For map[string]any contexts the key also includes the sorted exported key
54	// names, since patchTemplate output depends on which keys are present.
55	func templateCacheKey(rawText string, ctx any) string {
56		if ctx == nil {
57			return rawText
58		}
59	
60		t := reflect.TypeOf(ctx)
61		if t.Kind() == reflect.Map {
62			if m, ok := ctx.(map[string]any); ok {
63				keys := make([]string, 0, len(m))
64				for k := range m {
65					if r, _ := utf8.DecodeRuneInString(k); unicode.IsUpper(r) {
66						keys = append(keys, k)
67					}
68				}
69				sort.Strings(keys)
70				return rawText + "\x00" + t.String() + "\x00" + strings.Join(keys, "\x01")
71			}
72		}
73	
74		return rawText + "\x00" + t.String()
75	}
76	
77	// parsedTemplate returns a fully-parsed *template.Template for text.
78	// The first call for a given key parses and stores it; subsequent calls
79	// return the cached value. Concurrent first-renders of the same template
80	// may both parse, but LoadOrStore ensures only one result is shared.
81	func parsedTemplate(text *Text) (*template.Template, error) {
82		// Key on the raw, unpatched template text plus the context type so that
83		// cache hits can skip patchTemplate entirely: patching is only needed
84		// the first time a given (raw template, context type) pair is seen.
85		key := templateCacheKey(text.template, text.context)
86	
87		if cached, ok := parsedTemplates.Load(key); ok {
88			return cached.(*template.Template), nil
89		}
90	
91		// Cache miss: patch the raw template into its executable form.
92		text.patchTemplate()
93	
94		// Parse into a fresh template with the shared func map and settings.
95		tmpl, err := template.New("cache").Funcs(funcMap()).Parse(text.template)
96		if err != nil {
97			return nil, err
98		}
99	
100		// Store; if another goroutine already stored an equivalent template, use theirs.
101		actual, _ := parsedTemplates.LoadOrStore(key, tmpl)
102		return actual.(*template.Template), nil
103	}
104	
105	func (t *renderer) execute(text *Text) (string, error) {
106		tmpl, err := parsedTemplate(text)
107		if err != nil {
108			log.Error(err)
109			return "", errors.New(InvalidTemplate)
110		}
111	
112		t.context.init(text)
113	
114		err = tmpl.Execute(&t.buffer, t.context)
115		if err != nil {
116			log.Error(err)
117			return "", errors.New(IncorrectTemplate)
118		}
119	
120		output := t.buffer.String()
121	
122		// issue with missingkey=zero ignored for map[string]any
123		// https://github.com/golang/go/issues/24963
124		output = strings.ReplaceAll(output, "<no value>", "")
125	
126		return output, nil
127	}

1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/base64"
6		"encoding/gob"
7		"os"
8		"time"
9	
10		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/cache"
11		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/log"
12	)
13	
14	const (
15		configKey = "CONFIG"
16		SourceKey = "CONFIG_SOURCE"
17	
18		// envKey is resolved into the --config flag by the root command when no
19		// explicit flag is given. The shell's init script pins it to the session's
20		// resolved configuration source so the configuration can be recovered when
21		// the session cache is lost or corrupted. A healthy session cache always
22		// wins, so it can not be used to change the configuration mid-session.
23		envKey = "POSH_CONFIG"
24	)
25	
26	func (cfg *Config) Store() {
27		defer log.Trace(time.Now())
28	
29		cache.Set(cache.Session, SourceKey, cfg.Source, cache.INFINITE)
30		cache.Set(cache.Session, configKey, cfg.Base64(), cache.INFINITE)
31	}
32	
33	func Get(configFile string, reload bool) *Config {
34		defer log.Trace(time.Now())
35	
36		if reload {
37			log.Debug("reload mode enabled")
38			if source, OK := cache.Get[string](cache.Session, SourceKey); OK {
39				cfg := Load(source)
40				cfg.Store()
41				return cfg
42			}
43		}
44	
45		if base64String, found := cache.Get[string](cache.Session, configKey); found {
46			var cfg Config
47			if err := cfg.Restore(base64String); err == nil {
48				return &cfg
49			}
50	
51			log.Debug("failed to restore config from cache")
52			// the entry is corrupted beyond repair, drop it so subsequent renders
53			// skip the decode attempt and go straight to recovery
54			cache.Delete(cache.Session, configKey)
55		} else {
56			log.Debug("no cached config found")
57		}
58	
59		// A prompt render is invoked without an explicit --config and relies on the
60		// session cache for its configuration. When that cache is missing or corrupt
61		// - the file got purged (e.g. under disk pressure) or clobbered by a
62		// concurrent writer sharing the session - the shell session still knows its
63		// source: init exported it as POSH_CONFIG, and the root command resolved it
64		// into configFile. Store the parsed result again so the session cache
65		// self-heals. When the source is unavailable, return the default without
66		// caching it so the next render retries the recovery instead of pinning
67		// the default theme for the rest of the session.
68		if configFile != "" && configFile == os.Getenv(envKey) {
69			cfg, err := Parse(configFile)
70			if err == nil {
71				log.Debug("recovered configuration from environment: ", configFile)
72				cfg.Store()
73				return cfg
74			}
75	
76			log.Debug("failed to recover configuration from environment: ", configFile)
77			return Default(err)
78		}
79	
80		cfg := Load(configFile)
81		cfg.Store()
82		return cfg
83	}
84	
85	func (cfg *Config) Base64() string {
86		defer log.Trace(time.Now())
87	
88		var buffer bytes.Buffer
89		encoder := gob.NewEncoder(&buffer)
90		err := encoder.Encode(cfg)
91		if err != nil {
92			log.Error(err)
93			return ""
94		}
95	
96		return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(buffer.Bytes())
97	}
98	
99	func (cfg *Config) Restore(base64String string) error {
100		defer log.Trace(time.Now())
101	
102		data, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(base64String)
103		if err != nil {
104			log.Error(err)
105			return err
106		}
107	
108		var buffer bytes.Buffer
109		buffer.Write(data)
110		decoder := gob.NewDecoder(&buffer)
111		err = decoder.Decode(cfg)
112		if err != nil {
113			log.Error(err)
114			return err
115		}
116	
117		return nil
118	}

input.prompt: In D:\oh-my-posh\src\segments\plastic.go, Plastic.Init (line ~39) overrides Base.Init but only sets p.options and p.env — it never sets p.Segment = &Segment{} (see Base.Init in src/segments/base.go). Base.SetText/Text/SetIndex dereference b.Segment, so a plastic segment that becomes enabled (cm command installed) panics with a nil pointer dereference when the prompt renders and calls SetText. Fix Plastic.Init to call p.Base.Init(props, env) (or set Segment explicitly) while keeping its extra initialization, verify no other segment shadows Init the same way (grep for "func (.*) Init(props options.Provider, env runtime.Environment)" under src/segments/), and add a regression test that calls Init then SetText on the Plastic writer. Module root is src/.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: D:\oh-my-posh\.claude\skills\conventional-commit

# Conventional Commit

## Commit Message Structure

```text
<type>(<scope>): <description>

[optional body]

[optional footer(s)]
```

### Types

| Type       | When to use                                            |
| ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `feat`     | A new feature                                          |
| `fix`      | A bug fix                                              |
| `docs`     | Documentation changes, no code                         |
| `style`    | Formatting, missing semicolons, etc. (no logic change) |
| `refactor` | Code change that is neither a fix nor a feature        |
| `perf`     | Performance improvement                                |
| `test`     | Adding or correcting tests                             |
| `ci`       | CI configuration changes                               |
| `chore`    | Maintenance tasks (updating deps, tooling, etc.)       |
| `revert`   | Reverts a previous commit                              |

Append `!` after the type/scope to signal a **breaking change**: `feat!:` or `feat(api)!:`
When a change breaks existing behavior, **both markers are mandatory**: the `!` suffix on the type
**and** the `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer. They always appear together — never one without the other.

### Scope

Optional. Use the name of the area affected, e.g., `segment`, `cache`, `config`, `ui`.
Omit when the change is truly cross-cutting.

### Description

- Required. One short imperative sentence, no period at the end. The full header line (type +
  scope + description) must be **72 characters or fewer**. Aim for **50 characters or fewer for
  the description itself** — this almost always keeps the full header within budget regardless
  of type and scope length.
- Use the imperative mood: "add", not "added" or "adds". Never past tense or present-third-person:
  ✗ `added`, `fixed`, `bumped`, `implemented` → ✓ `add`, `fix`, `bump`, `implement`.
- **Never mirror the input's phrasing.** If the request uses past-tense words (`updated`, `added`,
  `bumped`, `was removed`, `got regenerated`), convert them to imperative before writing the
  description: `update`, `add`, `bump`, `remove`, `regenerate`.

### Body

Optional. Add context about _why_ the change was made, not _what_. The diff shows that.
Wrap at 72 characters.

### Footer

Use for:

- `BREAKING CHANGE: <description>` (required when `!` is used; explains the break).
- Issue references: `Closes #123`, `Fixes #456`.
- Co-authors: `Co-Authored-By: Name <email>`.

## Workflow

1. Run `git status` to review changed files.
2. Run `git diff` and `git diff --cached` to inspect staged and unstaged changes.
3. Identify the **type** from the table above. Ask yourself: does this change **remove, rename, or
   alter existing behavior** that callers depend on? If yes → it is a breaking change: use `!`
   after the type/scope **and** add a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer. Both markers are always required
   together.
4. Identify the **scope** from the files/area changed.
5. Write a short **description** in the imperative mood.
6. Add a **body** if the _why_ needs explanation.
7. Add a **footer** for breaking changes or issue references.
8. Stage the relevant files explicitly (avoid `git add -A`).
9. Commit with a message that preserves multi-line formatting when body/footer are present.

## Examples

```text
feat(segment): add Ramadan segment with Aladhan API
fix(cache): always store mod time
docs(readme): update installation instructions
refactor(config): simplify option parsing logic
chore(deps): bump github.com/shirou/gopsutil/v4
feat(segment)!: rename template property StartTime to Start

BREAKING CHANGE: template strings using .StartTime must be updated to .Start
```

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] Type is one of the allowed values in .commitlintrc.yml
- [ ] The commit message respects the rules defined in .commitlintrc.yml
- [ ] Scope (if present) reflects the actual area changed
- [ ] Description is imperative mood, no trailing period
- [ ] Full header line (type + scope + description) is 72 characters or fewer
- [ ] Both `!` after type/scope **and** `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer are present whenever the change breaks existing behavior
- [ ] No sensitive files staged (.env, credentials, etc.)

cd D:/oh-my-posh && git add src/config/segment.go src/config/segment_cache_test.go && git commit --amend -m "refactor(config): use generic cache for SegmentWriter

Store cached segment writers as gob-encoded snapshots instead of
JSON strings. Restoring decodes the snapshot into the writer
initialized by MapSegmentWithWriter, so its unexported runtime state
(env, options) survives. Replacing the writer instance dropped that
state, and any method relying on it panicked after a restore,
crashing the prompt process until the cache entry expired.

The snapshot also keeps cached state immutable: storing the live
writer would share one mutable object between render passes and the
cache. Legacy JSON entries are deleted on first read and re-cached
in the new format.

1	package cache
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"fmt"
6		"syscall"
7		"time"
8		"unsafe"
9	
10		"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/log"
11	)
12	
13	// Configuration constants
14	const (
15		minStringSize = 50 * 1024        // 50KB minimum string size
16		maxStringSize = 10 * 1024 * 1024 // 10MB maximum string size
17	)
18	
19	// Windows API constants
20	const (
21		fileMapAllAccess    = 0x001f001f
22		pageReadwrite       = 0x04
23		genericRead         = 0x80000000
24		genericWrite        = 0x40000000
25		createAlways        = 2
26		openExisting        = 3
27		fileAttributeNormal = 0x80
28		fileShareRead       = 0x00000001
29		fileShareWrite      = 0x00000002
30	)
31	
32	// Windows error codes surfaced via GetLastError.
33	const (
34		errorFileNotFound     = 2
35		errorSharingViolation = 32
36		sharingViolationTries = 3
37		sharingViolationSleep = 5 * time.Millisecond
38	)
39	
40	// Windows API functions
41	var (
42		kernel32           = syscall.NewLazyDLL("kernel32.dll")
43		createFileW        = kernel32.NewProc("CreateFileW")
44		createFileMappingW = kernel32.NewProc("CreateFileMappingW")
45		mapViewOfFile      = kernel32.NewProc("MapViewOfFile")
46		unmapViewOfFile    = kernel32.NewProc("UnmapViewOfFile")
47		closeHandle        = kernel32.NewProc("CloseHandle")
48		setFilePointer     = kernel32.NewProc("SetFilePointer")
49		setEndOfFile       = kernel32.NewProc("SetEndOfFile")
50		getFileSizeEx      = kernel32.NewProc("GetFileSizeEx")
51	)
52	
53	// PersistentSharedString represents a memory-mapped file for storing a single string
54	type PersistentSharedString struct {
55		filePath   string
56		fileHandle uintptr
57		mapHandle  uintptr
58		data       uintptr
59		size       int // Current allocated size
60	}
61	
62	func createOrOpenPersistentString(filePath string) (*PersistentSharedString, error) {
63		return createOrOpenPersistentStringWithSize(filePath, minStringSize)
64	}
65	
66	func createOrOpenPersistentStringWithSize(filePath string, requiredSize int) (*PersistentSharedString, error) {
67		// Ensure size is within bounds
68		if requiredSize < minStringSize {
69			requiredSize = minStringSize
70		}
71		if requiredSize > maxStringSize {
72			return nil, fmt.Errorf("required size %d exceeds maximum %d", requiredSize, maxStringSize)
73		}
74	
75		// First, try to open existing file
76		pss, err := openExistingFileWithSize(filePath, requiredSize)
77		if err == nil {
78			return pss, nil
79		}
80	
81		// If the file is locked, propagate so the caller falls back to
82		// in-memory-only use instead of recreating/truncating the file.
83		if errors.Is(err, ErrLocked) {
84			return nil, ErrLocked
85		}
86	
87		// File doesn't exist or too small, create new one with required size
88		return createNewFileWithSize(filePath, requiredSize)
89	}
90	
91	// openExistingFileWithSize attempts to open an existing memory-mapped file.
92	// The file is opened with FILE_SHARE_READ|FILE_SHARE_WRITE so concurrent
93	// oh-my-posh processes (split panes, tooltip renders, etc.) don't lock each
94	// other out. If the file is momentarily locked (ERROR_SHARING_VIOLATION) we
95	// retry briefly; if it's still locked after that we return ErrLocked so the
96	// caller can fall back to an in-memory-only store instead of recreating the
97	// file (which would truncate the other process's data).
98	func openExistingFileWithSize(filePath string, requiredSize int) (*PersistentSharedString, error) {
99		filePathPtr, err := syscall.UTF16PtrFromString(filePath)
100		if err != nil {
101			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to convert file path to UTF16: %v", err)
102		}
103	
104		var fileHandle uintptr
105		var lastErr syscall.Errno
106	
107		for attempt := 1; attempt <= sharingViolationTries; attempt++ {
108			var e error
109			fileHandle, _, e = createFileW.Call(
110				uintptr(unsafe.Pointer(filePathPtr)), // lpFileName
111				genericRead|genericWrite,             // dwDesiredAccess
112				fileShareRead|fileShareWrite,         // dwShareMode
113				0,                                    // lpSecurityAttributes
114				openExisting,                         // dwCreationDisposition
115				fileAttributeNormal,                  // dwFlagsAndAttributes
116				0,                                    // hTemplateFile
117			)
118	
119			if fileHandle != uintptr(0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF) { // INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE
120				break
121			}
122	
123			lastErr, _ = e.(syscall.Errno)
124	
125			switch uintptr(lastErr) {
126			case errorFileNotFound:
127				return nil, fmt.Errorf("file does not exist")
128			case errorSharingViolation:
129				if attempt < sharingViolationTries {
130					time.Sleep(sharingViolationSleep)
131					continue
132				}
133	
134				log.Debugf("cache file %s locked by another process after %d attempts", filePath, attempt)
135				return nil, ErrLocked
136			default:
137				return nil, fmt.Errorf("file does not exist")
138			}
139		}
140	
141		if fileHandle == uintptr(0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF) { // INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE
142			return nil, fmt.Errorf("file does not exist")
143		}
144	
145		// Get file size to check if it's large enough
146		var fileSize int64
147		ret, _, _ := getFileSizeEx.Call(fileHandle, uintptr(unsafe.Pointer(&fileSize)))
148		if ret == 0 {
149			_, _, _ = closeHandle.Call(fileHandle)
150			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get file size")
151		}
152	
153		actualSize := int(fileSize) - 5 // Subtract header (4 bytes length + 1 null terminator)
154		if actualSize < requiredSize {
155			// Existing file is too small, close and recreate
156			_, _, _ = closeHandle.Call(fileHandle)
157			return nil, fmt.Errorf("existing file is too small (%d < %d)", actualSize, requiredSize)
158		}
159	
160		return createMappingFromFileWithSize(filePath, fileHandle, actualSize)
161	}
162	
163	// createNewFileWithSize creates a new memory-mapped file with the specified size.
164	// The file is created with FILE_SHARE_READ|FILE_SHARE_WRITE so subsequent
165	// concurrent opens by other processes don't fail with a sharing violation.
166	func createNewFileWithSize(filePath string, size int) (*PersistentSharedString, error) {
167		filePathPtr, err := syscall.UTF16PtrFromString(filePath)
168		if err != nil {
169			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to convert file path to UTF16: %v", err)
170		}
171	
172		// Create new file
173		fileHandle, _, err := createFileW.Call(
174			uintptr(unsafe.Pointer(filePathPtr)), // lpFileName
175			genericRead|genericWrite,             // dwDesiredAccess
176			fileShareRead|fileShareWrite,         // dwShareMode
177			0,                                    // lpSecurityAttributes
178			createAlways,                         // dwCreationDisposition (overwrites if exists)
179			fileAttributeNormal,                  // dwFlagsAndAttributes
180			0,                                    // hTemplateFile
181		)
182	
183		if fileHandle == uintptr(0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF) { // INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE
184			if errno, ok := err.(syscall.Errno); ok && uintptr(errno) == errorSharingViolation {
185				log.Debugf("cache file %s locked by another process during create", filePath)
186				return nil, ErrLocked
187			}
188	
189			return nil, fmt.Errorf("CreateFileW failed: %v", err)
190		}
191	
192		// Set file size (4 bytes for length + size for string + 1 for null terminator)
193		totalSize := size + 5
194		_, _, _ = setFilePointer.Call(fileHandle, uintptr(totalSize), 0, 0) // FILE_BEGIN = 0
195		_, _, _ = setEndOfFile.Call(fileHandle)
196	
197		pss, mapErr := createMappingFromFileWithSize(filePath, fileHandle, size)
198		if mapErr != nil {
199			_, _, _ = closeHandle.Call(fileHandle)
200			return nil, mapErr
201		}
202	
203		// Initialize new file with empty string
204		basePtr := unsafe.Pointer(pss.data)
205		lengthPtr := (*uint32)(basePtr)
206		*lengthPtr = 0
207	
208		return pss, nil
209	}
210	
211	// createMappingFromFileWithSize creates a memory mapping from an open file handle with specified size
212	func createMappingFromFileWithSize(filePath string, fileHandle uintptr, size int) (*PersistentSharedString, error) {
213		totalSize := size + 5 // 4 bytes length + size + 1 null terminator
214	
215		// Create file mapping
216		mapHandle, _, err := createFileMappingW.Call(
217			fileHandle,         // hFile
218			0,                  // lpAttributes (NULL)
219			pageReadwrite,      // flProtect
220			0,                  // dwMaximumSizeHigh
221			uintptr(totalSize), // dwMaximumSizeLow
222			0,                  // lpName (NULL for unnamed mapping)
223		)
224	
225		if mapHandle == 0 {
226			return nil, fmt.Errorf("CreateFileMappingW failed: %v", err)
227		}
228	
229		// Map view of file
230		data, _, err := mapViewOfFile.Call(
231			mapHandle,          // hFileMappingObject
232			fileMapAllAccess,   // dwDesiredAccess
233			0,                  // dwFileOffsetHigh
234			0,                  // dwFileOffsetLow
235			uintptr(totalSize), // dwNumberOfBytesToMap
236		)
237	
238		if data == 0 {
239			_, _, _ = closeHandle.Call(mapHandle)
240			return nil, fmt.Errorf("MapViewOfFile failed: %v", err)
241		}
242	
243		return &PersistentSharedString{
244			filePath:   filePath,
245			fileHandle: fileHandle,
246			mapHandle:  mapHandle,
247			data:       data,
248			size:       size,
249		}, nil
250	}
251	
252	// SetString stores a string in the memory-mapped file (automatically persisted)
253	func (pss *PersistentSharedString) SetString(value string) error {
254		strBytes := []byte(value)
255	
256		if len(strBytes) > pss.size {
257			return fmt.Errorf("string too large for allocated space (%d > %d)", len(strBytes), pss.size)
258		}
259	
260		basePtr := unsafe.Pointer(pss.data)
261	
262		// Write length as first 4 bytes (little-endian)
263		lengthPtr := (*uint32)(basePtr)
264		*lengthPtr = uint32(len(strBytes))
265	
266		// Write string data starting at offset 4
267		if len(strBytes) > 0 {
268			stringPtr := unsafe.Add(basePtr, 4)
269			stringSlice := unsafe.Slice((*byte)(stringPtr), len(strBytes))
270			copy(stringSlice, strBytes)
271		}
272	
273		// Write null terminator
274		nullPtr := (*byte)(unsafe.Add(basePtr, 4+len(strBytes)))
275		*nullPtr = 0
276	
277		// No need to explicitly flush - Windows handles this automatically
278		return nil
279	}
280	
281	func (pss *PersistentSharedString) bytes() []byte {
282		basePtr := unsafe.Pointer(pss.data)
283	
284		// Read length from first 4 bytes
285		lengthPtr := (*uint32)(basePtr)
286		length := *lengthPtr
287	
288		if length == 0 {
289			log.Debug("empty string")
290			return []byte{0}
291		}
292	
293		if length > uint32(pss.size) {
294			log.Error(fmt.Errorf("corrupted data: length %d exceeds allocated size %d", length, pss.size))
295			return []byte{0}
296		}
297	
298		// Read string data starting at offset 4
299		stringPtr := unsafe.Add(basePtr, 4)
300		stringSlice := unsafe.Slice((*byte)(stringPtr), length)
301	
302		// Convert to string
303		result := make([]byte, length)
304		copy(result, stringSlice)
305		return result
306	}
307	
308	// Close closes the memory-mapped file and handles
309	func (pss *PersistentSharedString) close() error {
310		var err error
311	
312		if pss.data != 0 {
313			if ret, _, e := unmapViewOfFile.Call(pss.data); ret == 0 {
314				err = fmt.Errorf("UnmapViewOfFile failed: %v", e)
315			}
316			pss.data = 0
317		}
318	
319		if pss.mapHandle != 0 {
320			if ret, _, e := closeHandle.Call(pss.mapHandle); ret == 0 {
321				if err == nil {
322					err = fmt.Errorf("CloseHandle (mapping) failed: %v", e)
323				}
324			}
325			pss.mapHandle = 0
326		}
327	
328		if pss.fileHandle != 0 {
329			if ret, _, e := closeHandle.Call(pss.fileHandle); ret == 0 {
330				if err == nil {
331					err = fmt.Errorf("CloseHandle (file) failed: %v", e)
332				}
333			}
334			pss.fileHandle = 0
335		}
336	
337		return err
338	}

package cli

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/cache"
	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/prompt"
	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/runtime"
	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/shell"
	"github.com/jandedobbeleer/oh-my-posh/src/template"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
)

// streamCmd represents the stream command
var streamCmd = createStreamCmd()

func init() {
	RootCmd.AddCommand(streamCmd)
}

func createStreamCmd() *cobra.Command {
	streamCmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "stream",
		Short: "Stream the prompt with incremental updates",
		Long: `Stream the primary prompt with incremental updates as segments complete.
Output format: null-byte delimited prompt strings (each complete prompt separated by \0).
This allows multi-line prompts to be handled correctly.
Records prefixed with a record separator byte (\x1e) carry the transient prompt,
which shells cache so no additional CLI call is needed on line acceptance.
The shell can read records incrementally and update the display.
Command exits when all segments are resolved.`,
		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
		Run: func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string) {
			if shellName == "" {
				shellName = shell.GENERIC
			}

			flags := &runtime.Flags{
				ConfigPath:    configFlag,
				PWD:           pwd,
				PSWD:          pswd,
				ErrorCode:     status,
				PipeStatus:    pipestatus,
				ExecutionTime: timing,
				StackCount:    stackCount,
				TerminalWidth: terminalWidth,
				Eval:          eval,
				Shell:         shellName,
				ShellVersion:  shellVersion,
				Plain:         plain,
				Type:          prompt.PRIMARY,
				Cleared:       cleared,
				NoExitCode:    noStatus,
				Column:        column,
				JobCount:      jobCount,
				IsPrimary:     true,
				Escape:        escape,
				Force:         force,
				Streaming:     true,
			}

			options := []cache.Option{}
			if saveCache {
				options = append(options, cache.Persist)
			}

			cache.Init(shellName, options...)

			eng := prompt.New(flags)

			defer func() {
				template.SaveCache()
				cache.Close()
			}()

			// Stream prompt updates
			for promptString := range eng.StreamPrimary() {
				fmt.Print(promptString)
				fmt.Print("\x00") // Null byte delimiter for multi-line prompts
			}
		},
	}

	streamCmd.Flags().StringVar(&pwd, "pwd", "", "current working directory")
	streamCmd.Flags().StringVar(&pswd, "pswd", "", "current working directory (according to pwsh)")
	streamCmd.Flags().StringVar(&shellName, "shell", "", "the shell to stream for")
	streamCmd.Flags().StringVar(&shellVersion, "shell-version", "", "the shell version")
	streamCmd.Flags().IntVar(&status, "status", 0, "last known status code")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&noStatus, "no-status", false, "no valid status code (cancelled or no command yet)")
	streamCmd.Flags().StringVar(&pipestatus, "pipestatus", "", "the PIPESTATUS array")
	streamCmd.Flags().Float64Var(&timing, "execution-time", 0, "timing of the last command")
	streamCmd.Flags().IntVarP(&stackCount, "stack-count", "s", 0, "number of locations on the stack")
	streamCmd.Flags().IntVarP(&terminalWidth, "terminal-width", "w", 0, "width of the terminal")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&cleared, "cleared", false, "do we have a clear terminal or not")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&eval, "eval", false, "output the prompt for eval")
	streamCmd.Flags().IntVar(&column, "column", 0, "the column position of the cursor")
	streamCmd.Flags().IntVar(&jobCount, "job-count", 0, "number of background jobs")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&saveCache, "save-cache", false, "save updated cache to file")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&escape, "escape", true, "escape the ANSI sequences for the shell")
	streamCmd.Flags().BoolVarP(&force, "force", "f", false, "force rendering the segments")

	// Hide flags that are for internal use only.
	_ = streamCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("save-cache")

	return streamCmd

content.text:
Done. Here's the full picture.

## Root cause of your blank prompt — confirmed with a repro

The PR stored `segment.writer` (the live object) directly in the cache. Segment writers gob-persist **only exported fields**, so when a writer came back from the on-disk device cache in a later process, `restoreCache` replaced the freshly-initialized writer with one whose unexported `env`/`options` were nil. I built the pre-fix binary and reproduced both failure modes in this repo:

- **Streaming (your pwsh setup):** after restore, `Execute` continues and calls `writer.Enabled()` → nil-`env` dereference → panic in the segment goroutine, which has no `recover` — the whole process dies mid-render. That's the blank prompt. And because the poisoned entry sits in the device cache with the segment's TTL, **every subsequent prompt crashes the same way** — the "no way to recover" part. The old binary's second `stream` run panics in `Git.Enabled()` with a full stack trace; the fixed binary renders normally.
- **Non-streaming:** template method calls on the restored writer fail, rendering "unable to create text based on template" instead of the segment.

A secondary defect: the cached value was a *shared mutable pointer* — render passes mutate it (`SetText`/`SetIndex`), so the cached "snapshot" wasn't a snapshot, with race potential in the serve daemon.

## The fix (amended into the PR commit, author preserved: `26f8ed17`)

[segment.go](src/config/segment.go:409): `setCache` now stores a **gob-encoded `[]byte` snapshot** of the writer; `restoreCache` decodes it **into** the writer `MapSegmentWithWriter` just initialized (gob leaves untouched fields alone), so `env`/`options` survive — the same overlay semantics the old JSON code had. Encode failures skip only that segment instead of poisoning the store. Legacy JSON `string` entries and interim live-writer entries both hit the delete-and-re-execute paths, so **anyone who ran the broken build self-heals on first prompt** (verified against the actual poisoned cache from the repro).

## Review comments — all addressed

- **env/options dropped on restore** (Copilot, the big one): fixed as above.
- **Shared mutable writer in cache**: fixed via the immutable snapshot.
- **`Pending` check**: was already restored; kept.
- **Type-switch instead of double lookup, legacy key via `cacheKeyAndStore`**: already in place; kept.
- **Test issues** ([segment_cache_test.go](src/config/segment_cache_test.go)): global `template.Cache` now saved/restored, `/tmp` → `t.TempDir()`, the inaccurate "writer needed for key" comment removed, and new subtests assert the restored writer is the *same instance* (`assert.Same`) plus snapshot immutability and legacy/unexpected-entry cleanup.
- **"marchalling" typo**: the whole stale TODO is gone, replaced by a comment explaining why the snapshot exists.

Verification: `go build`, full `go test ./...`, `golangci-lint` (0 issues), plus the end-to-end A/B above — second run logs `restored segment from cache: Git` and renders `{{ .StashCount }}` (env-dependent) correctly.

## Two things for you

1. **Push:** the branch is ready locally but I didn't push — it's a rewritten commit going to Mertozturkk's fork (`maintainerCanModify` is true). If you're happy with it: `git push git@github.com:Mertozturkk/oh-my-posh.git refactor/generic-segment-writer --force-with-lease`.
2. **Side finding (out of scope, chip spawned):** `Plastic.Init` in [plastic.go:39](src/segments/plastic.go:39) shadows `Base.Init` without setting `Base.Segment`, so an enabled Plastic segment nil-panics on `SetText` — same blank-prompt class of bug, independent of this PR.

Base.SetText/Text/SetIndex dereference b.Segment, so a plastic segment that becomes enabled (cm command installed) panics with a nil pointer dereference when the prompt renders and calls SetText. content.text:
Fixed and committed on its own branch, `fix/plastic-init-base-segment` (`7b23722c`), off `main` — the PR branch stays clean and is checked out again.

**What I did:**

- **Fix:** deleted the `Plastic.Init` override in [plastic.go](src/segments/plastic.go) entirely rather than patching it. It was a leftover from before `Base` carried per-segment state — it only re-did what `Base.Init` does, minus the `Segment = &Segment{}` initialization. With the override gone, the promoted `Base.Init` runs and sets everything. Also removed the two imports that became unused.
- **Shadow check:** grepped `src/segments/` for other `Init(props options.Provider, env runtime.Environment)` overrides — `Plastic` was the only one.
- **Regression test:** `TestPlasticInitializesBaseSegment` in [plastic_test.go](src/segments/plastic_test.go) calls `Init` then `SetText`/`SetIndex`/`Text`. I verified it catches the bug: against the old code it panics with the exact nil pointer dereference; against the fix it passes.
- **Verification:** `go build`, full `segments` package tests, and `golangci-lint` (0 issues) all green.

Note: a background-task chip for this same fix was already started, so that session is redundant — the work is complete here. When you want it upstream: `git push origin fix/plastic-init-base-segment` and open the PR.
